figures.caption: Figure 1: Overall framework of our proposed STAMBRIDGE model, illustrating the Spectral-Temporal Amplitudeaware Modulation (STAM) and the Mid-Feature Semantic Bridge (MFSB).

Table 2: Cross-model generalization of the Mid-Feature Semantic Bridge. Integrating the bridge consistently improves both Top-1 and Top-5 accuracy across different baseline architectures. Results are reported as mean ± std across 10 subjects.

Figure 3: t-SNE visualization of Image, EEG, and Text features. STAMBRIDGE successfully aligns EEG features into semantically consistent clusters that correspond closely to the visual and textual manifolds.

Figure 4: Top-10 zero-shot image retrieval results based on unseen EEG queries. The green boxes indicate correct matches (Top-1). Even for incorrect Top-1 retrievals, the subsequent candidates exhibit highly consistent semantics, demonstrating the robustness of our learned representations.

Table 4: Quantitative comparison of generative performance across all subjects. Our framework yields competitive or superior high-level semantic metrics (AlexNet, Inception, SwAV) compared to state-of-the-art baselines. Results for STAMBRIDGE are reported as mean ± std across 10 subjects.
Figure 5: Qualitative results of semantic image reconstruction across different subjects. Each subject shows the Best, Median, and Worst reconstruction cases based on semantic similarity.
